1: A revelation of God: 'Then the LORD (YHWH) answered Job out of the whirlwind.' The return of the divine name YHWH — the first since the prose frame — marks the covenantal breakthrough. In Christ, YHWH speaks definitively and finally: Hebrews 1:1-2 'in the past God spoke to our ancestors through the prophets at many times and in various ways, but in these last days he has spoken to us by his Son.' The whirlwind theophany is the OT form; the Incarnation is the NT reality. Acts 2:2 the Pentecost wind from heaven carries the same theophanic vocabulary.
2: A revelation of God: 'Who is this who clouds my purpose (ʿĕṣāh) with words that have no knowledge?' The divine counsel (ʿĕṣāh) that cannot be clouded is the eternal plan. Isaiah 46:10 'my purpose (ʿĕṣātî) will stand; I will do all that I please'; Ephesians 1:9-11 'the mystery of his will... the purpose of him who works out everything in conformity with the purpose of his will.' Christ is the agent of this eternal purpose (Ephesians 3:10-11 'according to his eternal purpose that he accomplished in Christ Jesus our Lord').
3: A revelation of God: 'Brace yourself like a man (ʾĕzor-nāʾ ḵĕḡeḇer); I will question you and you must answer me.' The divine challenge to stand. Revelation 1:17 when John sees the glorified Christ, 'I fell at his feet as though dead. Then he placed his right hand on me and said: “Do not be afraid. I am the First and the Last.”' The divine challenge that reveals human inadequacy is met by the same Christ who then assures and restores the one who falls.
4: A direct anticipation: 'Where were you when I laid the earth's foundation?' Job was not there — but Christ was. Hebrews 1:10 quotes Psalms 102:25 directly of Christ: 'In the beginning, Lord, you laid the foundations of the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands.' Colossians 1:16 'all things have been created through him and for him. He is before all things.' The rhetorical question that diminishes Job's standing is answered by the pre-existent Son who stood with the Father at creation.
5: A revelation of God: 'Who determined its measurements — do you know?' The precision of creation's dimensions. Proverbs 8:27-29 Wisdom was present 'when he marked out the horizon on the face of the deep, when he established the clouds above and fixed securely the fountains of the deep.' The measuring that YHWH challenges Job about is exactly the work that Christ as Wisdom performed (1 Corinthians 1:24 'Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God').
6: A direct anticipation: 'Who laid its cornerstone (ʾeḇen pinnāh)?' The cornerstone of creation has a NT referent. Psalms 118:22 'the stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone' — applied to Christ in Matthew 21:42; Acts 4:11; 1 Peter 2:6-7; Ephesians 2:20 'built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, with Christ Jesus himself as the chief cornerstone.' The cosmological cornerstone that YHWH placed at creation is the same Christ who is the cornerstone of the new creation.
7: A type: 'When the morning stars sang together and all the divine beings (bĕnê ʾĔlōhîm) shouted for joy?' The celestial court's joy at creation is fulfilled in the joy before the Lamb. Colossians 1:16 'whether thrones or powers or rulers or authorities; all things have been created through him and for him' — the morning stars and sons of God who shouted for joy at creation were themselves created by the pre-existent Christ. Revelation 5:11-12 these same beings sing before the Lamb: 'worthy is the Lamb who was slain, to receive power and wealth and wisdom and strength and honor and glory and praise!'
8: A revelation of God: 'Who shut in the sea with doors when it burst out from the womb?' Christ's sovereignty over the sea is the NT demonstration of his creatorial authority. Mark 4:39 'he rebuked the wind and said to the waves, “Quiet! Be still!” And the wind died down and it was completely calm.' Matthew 8:27 'what kind of man is this? Even the winds and the waves obey him!' The disciples' question answers itself: the kind who was present when the sea burst out from the womb and set its bars and doors.
9: A revelation of God: 'When I made clouds its garment and thick darkness its swaddling cloth.' The newborn sea wrapped in divine garments — the Creator as cosmic midwife. Christ enters human birth wrapped in human swaddling clothes (Luke 2:7) — the Creator who swaddled the primordial sea is himself swaddled as an infant. The clothing of creation and the clothing of the Incarnation are both the Creator's acts of governance.
10: A revelation of God: 'When I fixed its boundaries and set its bars and doors.' The ḥōq (statute/decree) for the sea — the same legal vocabulary as Sinai. The God who gave the Torah to Israel gave the sea its statute at creation; both are governance by the divine Word. John 1:1-3 'in the beginning was the Word... through him all things were made' — the statutes of creation and covenant are both the Word's work.
11: A type: 'This far you may come and no farther; here your proud waves will stop.' Fulfilled directly in the Gospels: Matthew 8:26 Jesus 'rebuked the winds and the waves, and it was completely calm.' The disciples' awe ('what kind of man is this?') is the NT recognition that they are witnessing the Creator exercising original authority. The boundary set by YHWH at creation is enforced by Christ at sea — same authority, same word, same Lord.
12: A revelation of God: 'Have you ever commanded the morning since your life began, or told the dawn where to take its place?' The daily renewal of morning as divine command. Matthew 5:45 'he causes his sun to rise on the evil and the good' — Christ cites this to ground the command to love enemies: the Father's daily morning-governance is the model for impartial love. 2 Peter 1:19 'until the morning star rises in your hearts' — Christ himself is the final morning that his return brings.
13: A shadow: 'So that it would seize the earth's edges and shake the wicked out of it.' The morning-as-shaker removing the wicked is eschatological. Hebrews 12:26-27 'at that time his voice shook the earth, but now he has promised, “Once more I will shake not only the earth but also the heavens.” The words “once more” indicate the removing of what can be shaken.' Christ's return is the ultimate morning that shakes creation and removes everything that does not belong to the kingdom.
14: A revelation of God: 'The earth changes like clay under a seal; its features stand out like a garment.' The seal pressed into clay leaves the creator's impression. God's seal on the Son is explicitly mentioned in John 6:27: 'on him God the Father has placed his seal.' The earth bearing God's creative seal points to Christ as the one who bears the Father's seal — the one on whom the imprint of the Father's nature rests (Hebrews 1:3 'the exact representation of his being').
15: The theme: 'From the wicked their light is taken away, and the arm raised in violence shall be broken.' The removal of the wicked's light and power. John 1:5 'the light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.' The arm of violence that is broken is the Adversary's power: Hebrews 2:14 'so that by his death he might break the power of him who holds the power of death — that is, the devil.'
16: A revelation of God: 'Have you gone down to the springs of the sea, or walked through the depths of the abyss (tĕhôm)?' Christ descends to the depths. Philippians 2:10 'in heaven and on earth and under the earth every knee should bow' — the under-the-earth dimension is Christ's descent. 1 Peter 3:18-19 'he was put to death in the body but made alive in the Spirit, through whom also he went and made proclamation to the imprisoned spirits.' Christ walks through the depths that Job has never seen.
17: A direct anticipation: 'Have the gates of death been revealed to you? Have you seen the doors of deepest darkness (ṣalmāwet)?' Job has not seen these gates; Christ holds their keys. Revelation 1:18 'I am the Living One; I was dead, and now look, I am alive for ever and ever! And I hold the keys of death and Hades.' Matthew 16:18 'the gates of Hades will not overcome it [my church].' The gates that YHWH asks Job if he has seen are the gates that Christ has passed through, conquered, and now controls.
18: A revelation of God: 'Have you comprehended the earth's full breadth?' The spatial incomprehensibility of creation. Ephesians 3:18-19 'to grasp how wide and long and high and deep is the love of Christ, and to know this love that surpasses knowledge.' Paul applies spatial comprehension language to Christ's love — the breadth of the earth that Job cannot comprehend is surpassed by the breadth of Christ's love that Paul prays believers will grasp.
19: A revelation of God: 'Where is the way to where light lives?' John 1:9 'the true light that gives light to everyone was coming into the world'; 1 John 1:5 'God is light; in him there is no darkness at all.' The dwelling-place of light is the divine nature itself. John 8:12 'I am the light of the world' — Christ is the answer to the cosmological question: he is where light lives, having taken on flesh to bring the light's dwelling into the created world.
20: The theme: 'Can you take it to its territory and find the paths to its dwelling?' The inaccessibility of light's origin. The path to light's origin is Christ himself: John 14:6 'I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.' The path to where light dwells — to the Father — is the Son. The unanswerable question in Job is answered by the Incarnation.
21: A type (ironic): 'You know, of course — for you were born then, and the number of your days is very great!' The divine irony at Job's expense. But there is one who was 'born then' — Proverbs 8:22-25 'the LORD brought me forth as the first of his works... I was formed long ages ago, at the very beginning, when the world came to be'; John 8:58 'before Abraham was born, I am.' The one who was actually present at the foundation is Christ, whose 'days' are indeed very great — eternal.
22: A revelation of God: 'Have you gone into the storehouses of the snow, or have you seen the storehouses of the hail?' The divine reserve of meteorological judgment. Revelation 8:7 'there came hail and fire mixed with blood, and it was hurled down on the earth'; Revelation 16:21 'hailstones weighing about a hundred pounds each fell from the sky on people.' The stored hail deployed in judgment is what Rev sees in Christ's eschatological return — the creation-storehouses finally opened.
23: A revelation of God: 'Which I have kept in reserve for the time of trouble, for the day of battle and war.' The divine reserve for eschatological judgment. Revelation 19:11-16 'I saw heaven standing open and there before me was a white horse, whose rider is called Faithful and True. With justice he judges and wages war... He is dressed in a robe dipped in blood, and his name is the Word of God.' The reserved arsenal deployed at creation's end is deployed by the returning Christ.
24: A revelation of God: 'By what path does the light divide, or the east wind scatter itself across the earth?' The east wind (qādîm) as divine instrument. Matthew 24:27 'for as lightning that comes from the east is visible even in the west, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.' The east as the direction of divine approach connects the meteorological east wind to the eschatological direction of Christ's return.
25: A revelation of God: 'Who has cut a channel (pēleg) for the flood of waters, or a path for the thunderbolt?' The designed channels of rain and lightning. Psalms 1:3 'like a tree planted by streams of water (palgê-māyim)' — the same channel-vocabulary; the channels of water that YHWH cut at creation become the channels of blessing that produce the righteous person's fruitfulness. John 7:38 'whoever believes in me, as Scripture has said, rivers of living water will flow from within them' — the channels of the Spirit.
26: A revelation of God: 'To bring rain on a land where no person lives, on the desert with no human in it.' God's care for the uninhabited. Luke 12:6 'are not five sparrows sold for two pennies? Yet not one of them is forgotten by God' — Jesus extends the same principle: the Father cares for what is beyond human observation or appreciation. The rain on uninhabited desert is evidence of a Creator whose care is not anthropocentric.
27: A revelation of God: 'To satisfy the desolate wasteland and make the tender grass spring up.' The watered desert as image of divine abundance. Isaiah 35:1-2 'the desert and the parched land will be glad; the wilderness will rejoice and blossom' — the Messianic transformation of desert to garden. John 4:14 'the water I give them will become in them a spring of water welling up to eternal life' — Christ brings the spring that makes the wasteland bloom.
28: A revelation of God: 'Does the rain have a father? Who has fathered the drops of dew?' The Father-language for natural processes. Isaiah 9:6 'everlasting Father (ʾăḇî-ʿaḏ)' is a Messianic title for the coming child. The Father of rain is also the everlasting Father — both are functions of the same divine generativity that is expressed in Christ as eternal Son and applied to all creation.
29: A revelation of God: 'From whose womb did the ice come?' The birth-language for creation processes — ice born from a divine womb — is the complement to the male Father-of-rain imagery. Together they suggest that divine creativity transcends the gender categories it uses. Colossians 1:15 'the firstborn of all creation' — Christ's relation to creation is expressed in birth-language; he is born 'before all creation' as the Father's eternal Son.
30: A revelation of God: 'The waters harden like stone, and the surface of the deep is locked.' The frozen deep is the most remote and inaccessible part of creation. Christ descended into the lowest depths (Ephesians 4:9 'he also descended to the lower, earthly regions'; Philippians 2:10 'under the earth'). The locked deep that YHWH challenges Job about is the depth that Christ has already entered and emerged from in the resurrection.
31: A revelation of God: 'Can you bind the chains of the Pleiades, or can you release the belt of Orion?' The constellations under divine governance. Colossians 1:16-17 'whether thrones or powers or rulers or authorities... in him all things hold together' — the stars are among the 'all things' that hold together in Christ. Amos 5:8 'he who made the Pleiades and Orion, who turns midnight into dawn' — the same Creator who governs the stars is praised in both testaments.
32: A revelation of God: 'Can you bring out Mazzaroth in its season? Can you guide the Bear with its cubs?' The seasonal guidance of constellations is part of the creation governance attributed to Christ: Hebrews 1:3 'sustaining all things by his powerful word.' The Bear guided through the seasons is held in the same Word by which Christ sustains all creation — from the largest constellation to the smallest sparrow (Matthew 10:29).
33: A revelation of God: 'Do you know the laws of heaven (ḥuqqôṯ šāmayim)? Can you establish its rule over the earth?' The ḥuqqôṯ (statutes/laws) of the heavens parallel the Torah's ḥuqqîm — both are expressions of divine legislative authority. Matthew 5:17 'do not think that I have come to abolish the Law or the Prophets; I have not come to abolish them but to fulfill them.' Christ who fulfills the Torah is the same who established the laws of heaven — both are his word.
34: A revelation of God: 'Can you raise your voice to the clouds and make a flood of water come down?' The prayer-to-cloud motif. John 11:41-43: before raising Lazarus, Jesus prays and then commands — the Father answers immediately. John 12:28 'Father, glorify your name! Then a voice came from heaven: “I have glorified it, and will glorify it again.”' Christ's prayer is immediately answered by the Father — not because he has human power to command rain, but because the Father honors the Son's voice.
35: A revelation of God: 'Can you send out lightning bolts on commission, and do they report back to you: Here we are?' The lightning as obedient messenger. Psalms 104:4 'he makes winds his messengers, flames of fire his servants' (quoted in Hebrews 1:7 of the angels). Revelation 22:6 'the Lord, the God who inspires the prophets, sent his angel to show his servants the things that must soon take place.' Christ sends his messengers — angels, prophets, apostles — who report back; he commands the lightning as he commands his church.
36: A direct anticipation: 'Who has placed wisdom within the hidden self (ṭuḥôṯ)?' The inward wisdom planted by God. Jeremiah 31:33 'I will put my law in their minds and write it on their hearts' — the New Covenant inward law. 1 Corinthians 2:10 'the Spirit searches all things, even the deep things of God'; 1 Corinthians 1:24 'Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God'; 1 Corinthians 1:30 'Christ Jesus... has become for us wisdom from God.' The wisdom hidden in the inner self is Christ himself, received through the Spirit's indwelling.
37: A revelation of God: 'Who has the wisdom to count the clouds? Who can tilt the water jars of heaven?' The cloud-counting and water-jar wisdom. 2 Corinthians 4:7 'we have this treasure in jars of clay' — the vessel-of-water metaphor is applied to the gospel bearers; they carry the divine water of the Spirit in clay jars. The water jars of heaven that only God can tilt are the source; the clay jars of the apostles are the vessels for distributing what God pours out.
38: A revelation of God: 'When the dust hardens into a mass and the clods of dirt cling together?' The agricultural reality of drought-hardened soil. The soil that only rain can soften images the human heart that only the Spirit can soften (Ezekiel 36:26 'I will remove from you your heart of stone and give you a heart of flesh'). The same divine power that tips the water jars of heaven to soften the dust softens the hardened human heart.
39: A revelation of God: 'Do you hunt prey for the lion and fill the hunger of its cubs?' The divine provision for the apex predator. Matthew 6:26 'look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them.' If God provides for birds and lions — creatures of no soteriological significance — how much more for human beings? Christ draws the a fortiori argument: the Creator who feeds the lion feeds his people.
40: A revelation of God: 'When they crouch in their dens and wait in ambush in their thickets?' The lion's behavior as part of the divinely ordered predator-prey system. Revelation 5:5 'see, the Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has triumphed' — Christ himself is the Lion, the apex predator of the spiritual order. The crouching lion that YHWH feeds is, in the eschatological reversal, Christ himself — the Lion who conquers by being the Lamb.
41: A revelation of God: 'Who provides the raven its food when its chicks cry out to God and wander about hungry?' The raven's cry to God for food. Luke 12:24 'consider the ravens: they do not sow or reap, they have no storeroom or barn; yet God feeds them. And how much more valuable you are than birds!' Jesus's application of the raven-feeding to human anxiety is the direct NT drawing-out of what YHWH's cosmogonic question implies: the God who hears the raven's cry is the same who hears the believer's cry — and values the believer infinitely more.
